text:
Agile methodology is a popular approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at the start, teams work in short cycles called **sprints**, usually lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint focuses on delivering a small, working part of the product.

Before a sprint, the team selects tasks from a **backlog**, which is a list of all the work that needs to be done. These tasks are often written as **user stories**, simple descriptions of a feature from the end user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a short **standup** meeting to share what they did yesterday, what they will do today, and any problems they face. This whole process is part of **iterative development**, where teams build, test, and improve the product gradually based on feedback. This flexibility helps teams respond quickly to changes.
